$comment: Reference case for exposure_ev (result = 1 / (2^ev * calibration)). Single coefficient `calibration` (the middle-grey factor) — it enters as a pure reciprocal scale on 2^-ev, so it is cleanly identifiable. Canonical calibration=1.0 differs from the library default 1.2 so the fitter recovers it from a cold start.
notes: 25-point sweep over exposure value ev ∈ [-3, 3]. The multiplier runs from ≈8 (ev=-3) down to ≈0.125 (ev=3). `calibration` is a global reciprocal scale, so the fit is well-conditioned and 2 % relative tolerance is tight.
